page.title: Subtle Modal Meanings
page.subtitle_html: Subtle Modal Meanings - C2 modal nuance for likelihood, possibility, advice, and necessity.
page.subtitle_text: C2 modal nuance for likelihood, possibility, advice, and necessity.

blocks.body: {"level":"C2","intro":"Subtle modal meanings: compact anchors for nuanced likelihood, tentative advice, theoretical possibility, and unnecessary past action.","rules":[{"label":"Likelihood","text":"Use may well, could well, and might just to express graded possibility.","example":"This may well explain the delay."},{"label":"Soft advice","text":"Use might want to, may be worth, and would be wise to reduce force.","example":"You might want to review the figures again."},{"label":"Past necessity nuance","text":"Contrast need not have with did not need to for past actions and necessity.","example":"They need not have changed the schedule."}]}

blocks.heading: C2 focus
blocks.body: This compact page anchors the Polyglot English C2 lesson on subtle modal meanings.
